titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 70

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, future as well as present, Duncan Earl of Fife sends Greeting: Let future as well as present know that I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Andrew the apostle and the canons serving God in that place, now and in the future, the chapel of Kettle, with land and with teinds, with oblations and with all things pertaining to the church of the chapel, to be held and possessed freely and quietly and without recovery by my heirs, in perpetual alms. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid canons should have and possess the aforenamed alms as freely and quietly, for the salvation of my soul and the souls of my ancestors and successors, as they hold and possess the rest of their alms in the kingdom of Scotia. As witnesses: Michael the clerk; Stephen the clerk; Hugh the earl’s clerk; Elias the clerk of Benholm; William the chaplain of Kirlymont; Odo the steward; William of Holderness; Richard Melville; Milo the earl’s man; Roger of Thorpe.
